system.name: Mythwright
system.description: Hunt the beasts of the foe folios, harvest their bodies, and smith their parts into SRD gear or creature-themed bespoke items.

recipeItemDefinitions.name: Mythwright Crafter's Handbook
recipeItemDefinitions.description: "Anyone can swing a hammer. A mythwright knows what the beast was for."A well-thumbed guild handbook, copied out in the cramped hand of Sera Vance, Guildmythwright of the frontier lodge. Between its patterns for smelting, smithing, fletching, leatherwork, and field repair are her marginal notes â€” which scales hold an edge, which bile eats through a tanning vat, how to tell a flawed temper before it costs you a finger. Owning it grants the baseline Mythwright crafting knowledge a working artisan is expected to have.While you carry this handbook, the standard mundane Mythwright recipes are available to you.

components.name: Slain Balehound Pup
components.description: The acid-soaked carcass of a young balehound â€” small enough for one harvester to carry slung over the shoulder, but already brittle and prone to leaking mordant if handled roughly.Harvest charges. 2.

components.name: Slain Balehound
components.description: The full-grown carcass of a balehound. Pincer-jaws fixed open in rictus, mordant glands still warm. A practiced harvester can pull several useful parts from one body before the acid eats them.Harvest charges. 4.

components.name: Slain Balehound Stalker
components.description: A lean, lithe balehound stalker â€” built for ambush, with longer sensory ears and a tighter mordant reserve than a base balehound. The hide is intact and the cartilage is prime.Harvest charges. 4.

components.name: Slain Balehound Belcher
components.description: A bloated belcher carcass â€” its throat and stomach swollen with caustic gas. Vent the gut first or the whole body slumps into a puddle on the floor.Harvest charges. 5.

components.name: Slain Balehound Fumigator
components.description: A fumigator carcass, its specialised exhalation glands intact. Treat the lungs with care â€” they remain pressurised even after death.Harvest charges. 5.

components.name: Slain Balehound Alpha
components.description: A hulking alpha balehound. The hide, teeth, and glands are all oversized; a single body keeps a team of harvesters busy for the better part of a day.Harvest charges. 6.

components.name: Slain Balehound Ironjaw
components.description: An ironjaw carcass â€” the namesake reinforced jawbones still capable of biting through stone if struck against an anvil. Salvageable as a smithing fixture or sundered into siege-grade fittings.Harvest charges. 6.

components.name: Slain Balehound Denmother
components.description: A denmother carcass: the largest balehound in the pack, with a thick hide and oversized brood-glands. Yields the most ingredients of any common balehound; reserve it for the most demanding crafts.Harvest charges. 7.

components.name: Slain JadmÃ³r Hatchling
components.description: A jadmÃ³r hatchling â€” three thrashing heads now still. The scales are supple and the fangs are still soft enough to carve. Easy to harvest from but the yields are modest.Harvest charges. 3.

components.name: Slain Mire JadmÃ³r
components.description: A mire-dwelling jadmÃ³r: four heads on a stout, lichen-darkened body. The lung-glands are unusually pressurised â€” handle with poisoner-grade care.Harvest charges. 5.

components.name: Slain Spitting JadmÃ³r
components.description: A spitting jadmÃ³r corpse. The specialised throat-glands at each head still hold ranged venom â€” sever them before any other harvest or the carcass loses half its value.Harvest charges. 5.

components.name: Slain JadmÃ³r
components.description: A full adult jadmÃ³r. Five heads, broad scale plates, generous venom reserves. The standard hunt-trophy carcass against which harvest tables are calibrated.Harvest charges. 6.

components.name: Slain JadmÃ³r Broodmother
components.description: A broodmother â€” six heads and a parasite-rich underside that should be flensed first by a butcher with a strong stomach. The brood-glands beneath the scales yield ingredients no smaller jadmÃ³r provides.Harvest charges. 8.

components.name: Slain Blackwater Tyrant
components.description: The carcass of an ancient Blackwater Tyrant. Ten heads, sword-length fangs, an ancient heart that still twitches when touched. A single body sustains a workshop for weeks.Harvest charges. 10.

components.name: Iron Ingot
components.description: A standard bar of refined iron. Smithing stock for most basic weapons and heavy armour.

components.name: Steel Ingot
components.description: A finer ingot of forge-hardened steel. Used for masterwork weapons and martial armour.

components.name: Wood Plank
components.description: A length of seasoned hardwood. Hafts, hilts, bowstaves, shaft stock.

components.name: Leather Hide
components.description: A large square of tanned leather. Sufficient for one armour panel or several smaller pieces.

components.name: Leather Strap
components.description: A narrow strap of tanned leather. Wraps, grips, fittings.

components.name: Cloth Bolt
components.description: A bolt of plain-woven cloth. Padding, lining, gambeson stock.

components.name: String Bundle
components.description: A bundle of strong cord, sinew, or hemp. Bowstrings, lashings, fletching wraps.

components.name: Fletching Feathers
components.description: A bundle of stiff, straight feathers â€” the vanes of arrows and bolts.

components.name: Iron Ore
components.description: A chunk of unrefined iron ore, broken from a seam and roughly cleaned. Useless on its own â€” must be smelted before it can be worked into ingots or finished gear.

components.name: Miner's Pick
components.description: A heavy steel pickaxe sized for ore-work and tunnel-cutting. The head is single-pointed for stone, with a hammer claw at the back for splitting seams.It functions as a simple martial weapon and as the mining tool used by gathering tasks in Mythwright's mine environments.

components.name: Acid-Proof Hide
components.description: Scraps and panels of balehound hide, naturally immune to strong acids thanks to the creature's mordant-soaked physiology. Tough and chemically inert, it is sought by alchemists and artificers who must handle dangerous reagents.Source. Harvested with a successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check using alchemist's supplies, poisoner's tools, glass vials, or butcher's tools. A Balehound yields acid-proof hide scraps at DC 12; an Alpha or Ironjaw yields a large acid-proof hide at DC 15; and a Balehound Denmother yields enough acid-proof hide for a major crafting project at DC 17. A failed check deals acid damage equal to the creature's rupture damage dice.Suggested crafting uses. Gloves, satchels, vial harnesses, alchemist aprons, or lining for containers that hold dangerous reagents.

components.name: Balehound Mordant
components.description: A potent, sticky, caustic substance harvested from the acid glands of a balehound, vialed for transport. Balehound Mordant readily dissolves flesh, bone, metal and stone, yet leaves glass untouched.Source. Drawn from any balehound. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (alchemist's supplies, poisoner's tools, glass vials, or butcher's tools) yields vials of mordant. Typical harvest DCs run from 10 (Balehound Pup, a minor vial of weak mordant) and 12 (Balehound, 1d2 vials) up through 14 (Fumigator, Belcher, or Stalker, 1d3 vials), 15 (Alpha or Ironjaw, 1d4 vials), and 17 (Denmother, 2d4 vials). On a failed check the harvester takes acid damage equal to the creature's rupture damage dice.Suggested crafting uses. Acid flasks, dissolving paste, lock-eating solvent, alchemical etching, monster-lure, trap payloads, or ammunition coating.

components.name: Balehound Teeth
components.description: A bag of curved, mismatched teeth pried from a balehound's pincer-shaped jaws, packed tight in rows at the snout. Hard, sharp, and acid-etched, they make excellent serrated fittings.Source. Balehound teeth are harvested with a successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check using alchemist's supplies, poisoner's tools, glass vials, or butcher's tools. A Balehound Pup yields one small bag of teeth at DC 10; a Balehound yields a bag of teeth at DC 12; and an Alpha or Ironjaw yields a large bag of teeth, plus a reinforced jawbone capable of biting through stone, at DC 15. A failed check deals acid damage equal to the creature's rupture damage dice.Suggested crafting uses. Serrated weapon fittings, armour-piercing traps, mining tools, or siege-sabotage equipment.

components.name: Ear Cartilage
components.description: A usable sensory organ taken from a balehound: long, complex ears filled with deep, ribbed channels that capture sound from every direction. Intact, they are the prize of monster-hunters seeking echolocation components.Source. Ear cartilage is among the yields from a Balehound (Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check, DC 12), and a usable sensory organ is a typical harvest from a Balehound Fumigator, Belcher, or Stalker at DC 14 - a hunter's bounty specifically prizes an intact stalker ear. Appropriate tools are alchemist's supplies, poisoner's tools, glass vials, or butcher's tools. A failed check deals acid damage equal to the creature's rupture damage dice.Suggested crafting uses. Echolocation charms, blindsight-themed magic items, alarm wards, or tremor-sensitive hunting tools.

components.name: Mordant Glands
components.description: The specialised acid-producing glands of a balehound, carefully excised intact. They continue to weep mordant after death, and careless butchery can turn the harvest into a smoking hole in the ground.Source. A specialised gland is a typical yield from the larger pack specialists, harvested with a successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check using alchemist's supplies, poisoner's tools, glass vials, or butcher's tools. Harvest DC 14 from a Balehound Fumigator, Belcher, or Stalker; DC 17 from the Balehound Denmother, who yields complete mordant glands. A failed check inflicts acid damage equal to the creature's rupture damage dice.Suggested crafting uses. Acid-resistant potion bases, volatile bombs, smokescreens, or caustic spell components.

components.name: Ancient Heart
components.description: The vast, centuries-old heart of a Blackwater Tyrant. It continues to beat slowly for days after the creature's death, suffused with regenerative power far beyond that of any lesser jadmÃ³r.Source. Harvested from a Blackwater Tyrant (Harvest DC 21).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (butcher's tools recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes 42 (12d6) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. A supreme reagent for regeneration magic, death-prevention talismans, cursed vitality items, revenant rites, and weapons that drain poison damage from a victim and return it as temporary healing to the wielder.

components.name: Blackwater Bile
components.description: The intensely caustic, corpse-pickling bile of a Blackwater Tyrant. Centuries of feeding on preserved corpses have made it a near-perfect solvent and embalming agent.Source. Harvested from a Blackwater Tyrant (Harvest DC 21).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (alchemist's supplies and glass vials recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes 42 (12d6) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Powerful acid flasks, corpse preservation, alchemical solvents, trap payloads, and embalming reagents.

components.name: Brood Glands
components.description: The swollen reproductive and spawning glands of a JadmÃ³r Broodmother, heavy with egg-slime and the chemical signals that draw parasites and tadpoles to a brood pool.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r Broodmother (Harvest DC 19).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools. On a failed check the harvester takes 28 (8d6) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour; a failure by 5 or more also inflicts a vivid hallucination and disadvantage on Wisdom checks for 10 minutes.Suggested crafting uses. Lure and bait compounds, breeding-pheromone reagents, fertility or growth alchemy, and witchcraft components themed around spawning and corruption.

components.name: Hatchling Scales
components.description: The thin, supple green scales of a jadmÃ³r hatchling. Smaller and less armoured than an adult's plates, but flexible and naturally poison-resistant.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r Hatchling (Harvest DC 14).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (leatherworker's tools recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes 7 (2d6) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Light poison-resistant armour trim, waterproofing, swamp gear, gloves, and decorative inlay.

components.name: JadmÃ³r Bile
components.description: Acrid, corpse-pickling fluid drawn from a jadmÃ³r's gut, equal parts caustic acid and preserving agent. It dissolves flesh while keeping it from rotting.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r Hatchling (Harvest DC 14), a Mire or Spitting JadmÃ³r (Harvest DC 16), a JadmÃ³r (Harvest DC 17), or a JadmÃ³r Broodmother (Harvest DC 19).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools. On a failed check the harvester takes the listed failure poison damage and must succeed on the listed Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Acid flasks, corpse preservation, alchemical solvents, trap payloads, or embalming reagents.

components.name: JadmÃ³r Fangs
components.description: Long, recurved teeth pried from one of a jadmÃ³r's many heads. Each fang is hooked and serrated, built to seize prey and drag it toward deep water.Source. Harvested from a Mire or Spitting JadmÃ³r (Harvest DC 16), a JadmÃ³r (Harvest DC 17), or a JadmÃ³r Broodmother (Harvest DC 19).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools yields fangs alongside the creature's other materials. On a failed check the harvester takes the listed failure poison damage (14 (4d6) for a Mire or Spitting JadmÃ³r, 21 (6d6) for a JadmÃ³r, 28 (8d6) for a Broodmother) and must succeed on the listed Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Harpoons, grappling hooks, serrated blades, trophies, or monster-hunter tools.

components.name: JadmÃ³r Heart
components.description: The slow-pulsing, fist-sized heart of a jadmÃ³r. It twitches for hours after death, charged with the creature's regenerative vitality. A potent and dangerous reagent.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r (Harvest DC 17) or a JadmÃ³r Broodmother (Harvest DC 19).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (butcher's tools recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes the listed failure poison damage (21 (6d6) for a JadmÃ³r, 28 (8d6) for a Broodmother) and must succeed on the listed Constitution saving throw (DC 17 / DC 18) or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. A major reagent for regeneration magic, death-prevention talismans, cursed vitality items, revenant rites, or weapons that deal poison damage to a victim and return it as temporary healing to the wielder.

components.name: Juvenile Fangs
components.description: The smaller, still-sharp teeth of a jadmÃ³r hatchling. Less imposing than an adult's fangs but already hooked and serrated, and easier to work into tools.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r Hatchling (Harvest DC 14).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools. On a failed check the harvester takes 7 (2d6) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Smaller harpoons and grappling hooks, serrated knives, arrowheads, trophies, and monster-hunter tools.

components.name: Massive Fangs
components.description: The enormous, sword-length teeth of a Blackwater Tyrant. Each is a hooked, serrated weapon in its own right, hard enough to take an edge and large enough to forge into a polearm head.Source. Harvested from a Blackwater Tyrant (Harvest DC 21).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (butcher's or leatherworker's tools recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes 42 (12d6) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Greatweapon blades and polearm heads, heavy harpoons and grappling hooks, master-tier monster-hunter tools, and trophies.

components.name: Parasite Glands
components.description: Tiny venom and anticoagulant glands cut from jadmÃ³r parasites or strained from a tadpole swarm. Each holds only a trace of weak poison, but in quantity they are valuable to alchemists.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r Parasite (Harvest DC 10, yields 1 gland or a vial of weak poison/anticoagulant) or a JadmÃ³r Tadpole Swarm (Harvest DC 12, yields 1d3 glands).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools. On a failed check the harvester takes 2 (1d4) poison damage (Parasite, Poison Save DC 10) or 5 (2d4) poison damage (Tadpole Swarm, Poison Save DC 12) and must succeed on that Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Lesser poisons, anticoagulants, antidote reagents (used for making antitoxins), or bait.

components.name: Parasite-Rich Tissue
components.description: Slabs of broodmother flesh still crawling with the parasites that nest beneath her scales. The tissue is alive with weak venom and writhing vermin and must be handled with care.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r Broodmother (Harvest DC 19).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (butcher's tools recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes 28 (8d6) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. A source of live parasite glands and anticoagulants, bait, lesser poisons, and reagents for antitoxin or anticoagulant alchemy.

components.name: Preserved Blood
components.description: Dark, slow-clotting blood strained from a jadmÃ³r tadpole swarm. It carries the venom's decay-slowing properties and resists spoiling, making it a useful base for alchemy.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r Tadpole Swarm (Harvest DC 12).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (glass vials recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes 5 (2d4) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Corpse preservation, embalming reagents, minor healing or anticoagulant alchemy, and spell components.

components.name: Regenerative Blood
components.description: Dark, sluggish jadmÃ³r blood that clots into rubbery strands and continues trying to knit flesh back together even after the creature is dead. It must be sealed quickly before it congeals.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r (Harvest DC 17) or a Blackwater Tyrant (Harvest DC 21).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (glass vials are recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes the listed failure poison damage (21 (6d6) for a JadmÃ³r, 42 (12d6) for a Blackwater Tyrant) and must succeed on the listed Constitution saving throw (DC 17 / DC 20) or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Healing draughts, unstable regeneration potions for regrowing lost limbs, spell components, or grafting reagents.

components.name: Scale Plates
components.description: Broad, almond-shaped scales of moss-dark green, reflective beneath their lichen-like growth and naturally resistant to poison and rot. A large jadmÃ³r yields enough plates for a major crafting project.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r (Harvest DC 17), a JadmÃ³r Broodmother (Harvest DC 19), or a Blackwater Tyrant (Harvest DC 21); each provides enough scale plates for a major crafting project.Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (leatherworker's or butcher's tools recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes the listed failure poison damage (21 (6d6) for a JadmÃ³r, 28 (8d6) for a Broodmother, 42 (12d6) for a Blackwater Tyrant) and must succeed on the listed Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Poison-resistant armour, shields, waterproofing, swamp gear, or reinforced boat plating.

components.name: Specialised Throat Glands
components.description: The pressurised throat glands of a mire or spitting jadmÃ³r, specialised organs that let the creature project gouts of venom or corpse-pickling brine across the shoreline.Source. Harvested from a Mire or Spitting JadmÃ³r (Harvest DC 16).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (poisoner's tools recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes 14 (4d6) poison damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (Poison Save DC 15 for a Mire JadmÃ³r, DC 16 for a Spitting JadmÃ³r) or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Pressurised venom-sprayer mechanisms, spitting trap reservoirs, alchemical aerosol delivery, and potent poison or necrotic-brine reagents.

components.name: Thickened Tadpole Oil
components.description: A viscous, faintly luminous oil rendered from the bodies of a jadmÃ³r tadpole swarm. It is rich in weak venom and clings stubbornly to skin, blades, and water alike.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r Tadpole Swarm (Harvest DC 12).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools. On a failed check the harvester takes 5 (2d4) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour.Suggested crafting uses. Lesser poison coatings, waterproofing oils, slow-burning lamp fuel, lure bundles, and alchemical base reagents.

components.name: Tyrant Venom
components.description: The ancient, concentrated venom of a Blackwater Tyrant, distilled across centuries of poisoning the same waters. A single sealed vial is potent enough to taint a well or drop a war-beast.Source. Harvested from a Blackwater Tyrant (Harvest DC 21).Harvesting. A successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check with appropriate tools (poisoner's tools and glass vials strongly recommended). On a failed check the harvester takes 42 (12d6) poison damage and must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour; a failure by 5 or more also inflicts a vivid hallucination and disadvantage on Wisdom checks for 10 minutes.Suggested crafting uses. Apex-tier poisons, devastating venom bombs, weapon coatings, trap reservoirs, and a master reagent for antitoxin or hallucinogen alchemy.

components.name: Venom-Pickled Reeds
components.description: Reed stems steeped in jadmÃ³r venom until they yellow at the tips and turn bitter and sour. Gathered from a poisoned hunting ground rather than the creature itself, they carry the venom's hallucinogenic properties.Source. Gathered from the venom-burned reed beds, ditches, and foam patches of any jadmÃ³r's poisoned hunting ground or lair.Harvesting. A character can collect them with a successful Intelligence (Nature) or Wisdom (Survival) check using appropriate tools (herbalism kit recommended). Handling untreated reeds risks the same poisoning as harvesting the parent jadmÃ³r; treat poisoned water and foam patches as the relevant hazard.Suggested crafting uses. Hallucinogens, divination smoke, poison filters, witchcraft components, or lure bundles.

components.name: Venom Sacs
components.description: Swollen, leathery glands cut from beneath a jadmÃ³r's waterlogged hide, still pressurised with greenish preserving venom that slows decay and discourages scavengers.Source. Harvested from a JadmÃ³r Hatchling (1d2 sacs), a Mire or Spitting JadmÃ³r (1d4 sacs), a JadmÃ³r (2d4 sacs), or a JadmÃ³r Broodmother (3d4 sacs).Harvesting. A character harvests a jadmÃ³r with a successful Intelligence (Nature), Wisdom (Survival), or Dexterity check using appropriate tools (Harvest DC 14 for a Hatchling, 16 for a Mire or Spitting JadmÃ³r, 17 for a JadmÃ³r, 19 for a Broodmother). On a failed check the harvester takes the creature's listed failure poison damage and must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (Poison Save DC 13 / 15-16 / 17 / 18 respectively) or be poisoned for 1 hour; if the failure is by 5 or more, they also suffer a vivid hallucination and have disadvantage on Wisdom checks for 10 minutes.Suggested crafting uses. Potent poisons, antitoxin development, venom bombs, arrow coatings, or trap reservoirs.

components.name: Mordant-Etched Dagger
components.description: Uncommon magic item (requires attunement)A dagger of dark iron whose edge has been etched and re-quenched in balehound mordant. The patterning runs in razor scallops along the blade and leaves a thin acrid mist when drawn.Magic Weapon. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.Mordant Edge. On a hit, the target takes an additional 1d4 acid damage. The acid also etches metal and stone the blade strikes â€” sufficient force can score a shallow groove in iron or split sealing wax cleanly.

components.name: Acid-Proof Coat
components.description: Uncommon magic item (requires attunement)A long coat of balehound hide, tanned in mordant and stitched with hardened cartilage panels at the shoulders and forearms.Armour Class. 11 + your Dexterity modifier.Acid Resistance. While wearing this coat, you have resistance to acid damage.

components.name: Belcher's Maw Bomb
components.description: Uncommon magic itemA clay sphere packed with sealed balehound mordant glands. The seal breaks on impact and the contents combust against the air.Action. Hurl the bomb up to 30 feet at a point you can see. Each creature within 5 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 3d6 acid damage.The maw bomb is consumed on use.

components.name: Pup-Sized Buckler
components.description: Common magic itemA small round buckler covered in mordant-tanned hide from a balehound pup. Light enough for any duellist; the acid-soaked hide turns aside the worst of a corroding strike.Shield. While wielded, this buckler grants +2 AC. It is a light shield and can be held while wielding a one-handed weapon.

components.name: Pack-Hunter's Net
components.description: Uncommon magic itemA heavy hunting net woven from balehound stalker sinew and denmother hide cord. The cords contract when they meet warm skin.Net. Standard Net rules apply (5-ft range; Large or smaller target restrained on hit; freed by a DC 10 Strength check or 5 slashing damage, AC 10).Constricting. A restrained creature is also disadvantaged on its first attempt to escape â€” the cords tighten reflexively when first tested.

components.name: Fumigator's Censer
components.description: Rare magic item (requires attunement)A bronze censer chained at the belt, fed by a sealed reservoir of balehound fumigator gland tissue. When the wearer wills it, the censer exhales a thin acrid mist.Spellcasting Focus. While attuned, you may use the censer as an arcane focus.Choking Fog (1/day). As an action, you may release the censer for 1 minute, creating a 10-foot-radius cloud of acrid mist centred on you that moves with you. The area is lightly obscured. Each creature that starts its turn within the cloud must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of its turn.

components.name: Ironjaw Cleaver
components.description: Rare magic item (requires attunement)A battleaxe whose head is forged around a ridge of balehound ironjaw teeth, set in living acid-glaze. The teeth never dull.Magic Weapon. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.Sundering Bite. When you hit a non-magical metal or stone object with this axe, you ignore that material's damage threshold and have advantage on the Strength check to break or shatter it.

components.name: Hatchling Scale Buckler
components.description: Uncommon magic itemA round shield layered with jadmÃ³r hatchling scales. The scales still flex slightly under pressure, dispersing impact across the disc.Shield (+1). While wielded, this shield grants +3 AC (+2 base, +1 magical bonus).

components.name: Tadpole-Oil Lantern Oil
components.description: Common magic itemA flask of viscous, faintly luminous oil rendered from jadmÃ³r tadpole bodies. Burns clean and bright in a lantern.Lantern Oil. Functions as standard lantern oil but burns for 12 hours rather than 6. The light it casts has a pale green cast.

components.name: Venom-Strike Arrows (10)
components.description: Rare magic itemTen arrows, each treated with a coating of jadmÃ³r venom and capped with a slow-release reservoir behind the broadhead.Venom-Strike. When an arrow hits a creature, in addition to the normal arrow damage, the target must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take 2d6 poison damage and be poisoned until the end of its next turn.Each arrow is expended after the strike, treated or not.

components.name: Brood-Slick Robes
components.description: Rare magic item (requires attunement)Robes lined with jadmÃ³r broodmother gland tissue. The lining secretes a thin neutralising slick across the outer fabric.Armour Class. 11 + Dex modifier. Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks while worn.Slick of the Brood. You have advantage on saving throws against being grappled or restrained.

components.name: Sundered-Scale Plate
components.description: Very rare magic item (requires attunement)Plate armour reforged from massive jadmÃ³r scale plates over a steel chassis. The scales overlap so the wearer hisses faintly when they move.Plate (+1). AC 19 (18 base + 1 magical bonus). Disadvantage on Stealth.Scale Reflex. When you are hit by a melee weapon attack, you may use your reaction to halve the damage of that attack. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per long rest.

components.name: Tyrant's-Heart Talisman
components.description: Legendary magic item (requires attunement)A fist-sized talisman containing the still-twitching heart of a Blackwater Tyrant, sealed in preserved blood and bound in massive jadmÃ³r fangs.Heart of the Tyrant. While attuned, your hit point maximum increases by 20 and you have advantage on death saving throws.Slow Pulse (1/long rest). When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, the heart pulses once. You drop to 1 hit point instead and become poisoned for 1 hour.

components.name: Bone-Splinter Arrows (20)
components.description: Uncommon magic itemTwenty arrows fletched normally but tipped with shards of jadmÃ³r massive-fang. The bone holds an edge and bites deeper than steel.Magic Ammunition. +1 to attack and damage rolls when fired.Splinter on Impact. An arrow that hits any creature embeds a bone splinter; on a critical hit, the target also takes 1d6 ongoing piercing damage at the start of each of its turns until it (or another creature) spends an action to extract the splinter (DC 12 Medicine).

components.name: Woodcutter's Axe
components.description: A working camp axe used to fell hardwood and limb branches for crafting stock. Doubles as a martial weapon in a pinch.

components.name: Hunter's Skinning Knife
components.description: A narrow field knife with a stiff back and a wickedly thin edge â€” built to part hide from carcass cleanly and to recover trophies without spoiling them.

components.name: Forager's Kit
components.description: A leather pack of cloth bags, glass vials, snips, twine, a small mortar, and a notebook of common-plant illustrations. Lets the bearer process reeds, herbs, mordant pools, and other delicate finds without spoiling them.

components.name: Broken Miner's Pick
components.description: A cracked pick head and a split haft. The metal can be re-forged and the wood replaced.This item is broken and cannot be used until repaired.

components.name: Broken Woodcutter's Axe
components.description: A dull, nicked axe with a loose head. The eye has worked itself open; the haft will need re-fitting before another tree comes down.This item is broken and cannot be used until repaired.

components.name: Broken Skinning Knife
components.description: A chipped skinning knife whose edge can no longer take a clean field cut without tearing the hide.This item is broken and cannot be used until repaired.

components.name: Broken Forager's Kit
components.description: A scattered forager's kit â€” straps torn, vials shattered, twine soaked through. Needs new bindings and replacement glass before it's of any use again.This kit is broken or scattered and cannot be used until repaired.

components.name: Battleaxe Head
components.description: A forged and ground battleaxe head, edge taken but not yet hafted. The intermediate stage between raw steel and a finished weapon â€” fit it to a haft to complete the axe.

components.name: Glass Vial
components.description: A small blown-glass vial with a waxed stopper, blown from melted sand and a pinch of flux. Stable stock for poultices, tonics, and alchemical decoctions.

components.name: Rare Herbs
components.description: A bound sheaf of uncommon medicinal herbs â€” bloodleaf, marsh-clover, and bittergreen among them. Foraged from old-growth and wetland margins, they form the restorative base of finer poultices and tonics.

components.name: Broken Plate Armour
components.description: A suit of plate armour split at the seams â€” buckled pauldrons, a staved breastplate, snapped straps. Beyond field repair, but a patient smith with steel, rivets, and the right knowledge can reforge it. A failed reforge ruins the salvage entirely.

components.name: Acid-Bite Arrows
components.description: UncommonArrows whose broadheads are scored and packed with a stabilised acidic reagent that ruptures on impact.Acid-Bite. When an arrow hits a creature, in addition to the normal arrow damage the target takes 1d4 acid damage. Higher-quality batches keep their charge longer and bite harder. Each arrow is expended after the strike.

components.name: Regenerative Blood Poultice
components.description: UncommonA salve of jadmÃ³r regenerative blood thickened with rare herbs and sealed in a glass vial. Pressed to a wound, it knits flesh with unnatural speed.Use. As an action, apply the poultice to a creature. It regains 2d4 + 2 hit points. Finer batches restore more and steady the recipient against bleeding. One use per vial.

components.name: Flawed Battleaxe
components.description: A battleaxe that left the forge with an uneven temper and a poorly seated head. It cuts, but the balance is off and the edge dulls quickly.Flawed. You have disadvantage on the first attack roll you make with this weapon in each combat as you adjust to its poor balance.

components.name: Masterwork Battleaxe
components.description: UncommonA flawlessly balanced battleaxe, its head ground to a keen even edge and seated on a perfectly fitted haft. The work of a master smith having a very good day.Masterwork. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. (Non-magical masterwork quality; not a magic weapon for the purpose of overcoming resistance.)

components.name: Flawed Longsword
components.description: A longsword the forge nearly got right. The blade is sound enough to fight with, but the temper runs uneven and the grip was set in a hurry â€” it wants correcting every time you raise it.Flawed. You have disadvantage on the first attack roll you make with this weapon in each combat as you adjust to its poor balance.

components.name: Masterwork Longsword
components.description: UncommonA longsword forged on a good day by a smith at the top of their craft â€” the blade ground to a true even edge, the tang seated dead straight, the balance falling exactly where the hand expects it.Masterwork. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. (Non-magical masterwork quality; not a magic weapon for the purpose of overcoming resistance.)

components.name: Flawed Mace
components.description: A serviceable mace with a poorly pinned head. It hits hard enough, but the first swing of any fight tends to find the weapon shifting in your grip.Flawed. You have disadvantage on the first attack roll you make with this weapon in each combat as you adjust to its poor balance.

components.name: Masterwork Mace
components.description: UncommonA mace whose flanged head is forged and seated as one true piece, perfectly weighted to fall where you mean it to. Plain, brutal, and flawless.Masterwork. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. (Non-magical masterwork quality; not a magic weapon for the purpose of overcoming resistance.)

components.name: Flawed Spear
components.description: A spear with a head set slightly off-true on the haft. It throws and thrusts well enough once you have the feel of it â€” which costs you the opening of the fight.Flawed. You have disadvantage on the first attack roll you make with this weapon in each combat as you adjust to its poor balance.

components.name: Masterwork Spear
components.description: UncommonA spear balanced so cleanly it seems to want to fly true, the head forge-welded straight and the haft turned smooth and even along its whole length.Masterwork. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. (Non-magical masterwork quality; not a magic weapon for the purpose of overcoming resistance.)

components.name: Flawed Longbow
components.description: A longbow tillered a little unevenly, so the limbs don't quite pull as one. It shoots true once your arm learns its quirks â€” but not on the first loose.Flawed. You have disadvantage on the first attack roll you make with this weapon in each combat as you adjust to its poor balance.

components.name: Masterwork Longbow
components.description: UncommonA longbow tillered to perfection, both limbs bending in flawless sympathy, the draw smooth from first inch to full. It rewards a steady hand with a clean, certain loose.Masterwork. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon. (Non-magical masterwork quality; not a magic weapon for the purpose of overcoming resistance.)

components.name: Flawed Chain Shirt
components.description: A chain shirt with uneven links and a loose hang. It turns a blade as often as not, but the poor fit leaves gaps a careful foe can find.Flawed. This armour grants 1 less AC than a well-made chain shirt (AC 12 + Dex modifier, maximum 2).

components.name: Masterwork Chain Shirt
components.description: UncommonA chain shirt riveted link by link to fit its wearer exactly, every ring closed true so no gap opens however you move.Masterwork. You gain a +1 bonus to AC while wearing this armour (AC 14 + Dex modifier, maximum 2). Non-magical masterwork quality.

components.name: Flawed Shield
components.description: A shield with a warped board and a loose grip. It still stops a blow, but it sits wrong on the arm and you can't quite trust the angle.Flawed. This shield grants a +1 bonus to AC, rather than the +2 of a well-made shield.

components.name: Masterwork Shield
components.description: UncommonA shield of seasoned board and a true steel rim, the boss set dead centre and the grip fitted to the arm. It meets every blow square.Masterwork. This shield grants a +3 bonus to AC, rather than the +2 of an ordinary shield. Non-magical masterwork quality.

components.name: Mythwright Crafter's Handbook
components.description: "Anyone can swing a hammer. A mythwright knows what the beast was for."A well-thumbed guild handbook, copied out in the cramped hand of Sera Vance, Guildmythwright of the frontier lodge. Between its patterns for smelting, smithing, fletching, leatherwork, and field repair are her marginal notes â€” which scales hold an edge, which bile eats through a tanning vat, how to tell a flawed temper before it costs you a finger. Owning it grants the baseline Mythwright crafting knowledge a working artisan is expected to have.While you carry this handbook, the standard mundane Mythwright recipes are available to you.

components.name: Formula: Acid-Bite Arrows
components.description: A field diagram for treating arrowheads with a stabilised acidic reagent harvested from balehounds or blackwater jadmÃ³r. Owning this formula unlocks the Craft Acid-Bite Arrows recipe.

components.name: Formula: Reforge Broken Plate
components.description: A guild schematic for stripping, re-tempering, and reassembling ruined plate harness. Demanding work â€” a botched reforge destroys the salvage. Owning this formula unlocks the Reforge Broken Plate Armour recipe.

components.name: Formula: Regenerative Blood Poultice
components.description: Monster-research notes on stabilising jadmÃ³r regenerative blood into a usable healing salve before it congeals. Owning this formula unlocks the Brew Regenerative Blood Poultice recipe.

components.name: Bone-Shadow Remains
components.description: The collapsed frame of a Bone-Shadow Hatchling â€” Kuuvalgus's weakest reconstituted form, undone before it could grow. Black dragonbone and dried sinew remain, cold and faintly humming.Harvest charges. 3.

components.name: Fallen Incomplete Dracolich
components.description: The half-finished resurrection of Kuuvalgus, brought down before completion. A larger black dragonbone frame, threaded with necrotic marrow and the first true dracolich scales.Harvest charges. 5.

components.name: Sundered Reforged Bonemaw
components.description: The vast, near-complete dracolich felled and sundered. Wings of black dragonbone, a maw of needle fangs, and â€” caught in the ribcage â€” a splinter of his broken phylactery, still whispering.Harvest charges. 7.

components.name: Remains of Kuuvalgus the Vile
components.description: The complete dracolich at last laid to rest. The richest harvest in all Mythwright â€” pristine dragonbone, soul-ash by the handful, the largest phylactery shard, and, set where his heart should be, one of the Five Tears of Kuuvalgus.Harvest charges. 9.

components.name: Fallen Marrow Revenant
components.description: The slack, waterlogged remains of a marrow revenant â€” a Bonemarsh soldier the dracolich's necromancy raised and the party put down again. Dark sinew and gravesoaked bone are still worth taking, if you can stomach the smell.Harvest charges. 3.

components.name: Slain Bone-Shadow Drake
components.description: The crumpled frame of a bone-shadow drake â€” a lesser bone-dragon shed from Kuuvalgus's own remaking. Cold black dragonbone and overlapping dracolich scale make it the richest mid-tier hunt in the Bonemarsh.Harvest charges. 3.

components.name: Sundered Tear-Warden
components.description: What is left when a tear-warden's binding fails â€” a settling drift of phantom ichor over a husk of dragonbone, the phylactery shard at its heart finally going quiet. Harvest it with care and sealed glass.Harvest charges. 3.

components.name: Guttered Gravegloom Wisp
components.description: A gravegloom wisp leaves no body â€” only a slick of cold violet-gold residue where it guttered out, and a thimble of soul-ash. Scrape it into a vial quickly, before the mist forgets it was ever here.Harvest charges. 2.

components.name: Black Dragonbone
components.description: A length of Kuuvalgus's black dragonbone â€” light, cold to the touch, and harder than tempered steel. It rings faintly when struck and never quite stops.

components.name: Necrotic Marrow
components.description: Black ichor drawn from the hollows of dragonbone â€” the undeath that animates Kuuvalgus made liquid. It withers what it touches and must be handled in sealed glass.

components.name: Dracolich Scale
components.description: A broad obsidian-black scale from a reforged dracolich, edged like slate and proof against fire and fang alike. Heavy, but it turns blows that would split plate.

components.name: Phylactery Shard
components.description: A splinter of Kuuvalgus's broken phylactery, still humming with bound soul-stuff. Warded correctly it anchors enchantment; handled carelessly it whispers.

components.name: Soul-Ash
components.description: Fine grey ash left where a dracolich's animating fire guttered out. A pinch carries the cold memory of unlife â€” prized by alchemists and necromancers, feared by everyone else.

components.name: Tear of Kuuvalgus
components.description: One of the Five Tears â€” a crystallised drop of the gnome-wizard's grief and malice, the keystone of his reconstitution. Immensely potent and nearly impossible to win; only the fall of Kuuvalgus the Vile yields one.

components.name: Bonemaw Fang
components.description: A curved fang from a dracolich's maw, black at the root and needle-keen at the tip. Holds an edge no whetstone gave it.

components.name: Withered Sinew
components.description: Desiccated undead sinew that still twitches when the light fails. Dried and waxed it makes a bowstring or binding that simply refuses to fray.

components.name: Gravesoaked Bone
components.description: Battlefield bone drawn from a Bonemarsh revenant â€” soaked so long in Kuuvalgus's necromancy that it darkened to the colour of wet slate. It splinters into wicked, self-sharpening shards and holds a wardmark without flaking.

components.name: Phantom Ichor
components.description: Violet-gold mist condensed from the body of a phylactery-bound undead, caught in a sealed vial before it could disperse. It coils against the glass like something still trying to remember a shape. Necromancers prize it; everyone else buries it.

components.name: Formula: Mistbound Talisman
components.description: Guild schematics for setting a quieted phylactery shard in gravesoaked bone and sealing it with phantom ichor, so the soul-stuff within answers to the bearer instead of the dracolich. Owning this formula unlocks the Bind a Mistbound Talisman recipe.

components.name: Mistbound Talisman
components.description: Uncommon magic item (requires attunement)A disc of gravesoaked bone wound with a thread of quieted phylactery shard, the whole sealed under a bead of phantom ichor that turns slowly when the dead are near.Mistward. While attuned, you have advantage on saving throws against being frightened, and the first time each turn you take necrotic damage you reduce it by 5.

components.name: Dragonbone Greatsword
components.description: Rare magic item (requires attunement)A greatsword forged around a core of Kuuvalgus's black dragonbone, edged with a split bonemaw fang. Lighter than steel and unnaturally cold.Magic Weapon. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.Bonechill. Once per turn on a hit, the target takes an extra 1d6 necrotic damage.

components.name: Necrotic-Tipped Arrows
components.description: UncommonArrowheads steeped in necrotic marrow drawn from a dracolich's bones.Withering Shot. On a hit, the target takes an extra 1d6 necrotic damage and cannot regain hit points until the start of your next turn. Each arrow is expended after the strike.

components.name: Soul-Ash Bomb
components.description: UncommonA sealed glass flask of soul-ash and reactive reagents. Shatters into a cloud of unlife.Throw (range 20/60). Each creature in a 5-ft radius makes a DC 13 Constitution save, taking 3d6 necrotic damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. Single use.

components.name: Phylactery-Warded Charm
components.description: Rare magic item (requires attunement)A charm built around a splinter of Kuuvalgus's phylactery, bound in black dragonbone so the soul-stuff within stays quiet.Ward of Unlife. While attuned, you have resistance to necrotic damage and advantage on saving throws against being frightened by undead.

components.name: Dracolich-Scale Plate
components.description: Very rare magic item (requires attunement)Heavy plate built from overlapping dracolich scales over a steel chassis. Black, cold, and proof against the fire that should have ended its maker.Plate (+1). AC 19 (18 base + 1 magical bonus). Disadvantage on Stealth.Undying Scale. While attuned, you have resistance to necrotic damage and to fire damage.

components.name: Formula: Dragonbone Weapons
components.description: Trophy-study notes on working Kuuvalgus's black dragonbone into a weapon core without the marrow rotting the smith's hands. Owning this formula unlocks the Forge a Dragonbone Greatsword recipe.

components.name: Formula: Soul-Ash Munitions
components.description: Necromantic field notes on packing soul-ash and necrotic marrow into stable munitions. Owning this formula unlocks the Necrotic-Tipped Arrows and Soul-Ash Bomb recipes.

components.name: Formula: Phylactery Wards
components.description: Guild schematics for safely binding a phylactery shard into a protective charm. Owning this formula unlocks the Ward a Phylactery Charm recipe.

components.name: Formula: Dracolich-Scale Armour
components.description: Master armourer's notes on layering dracolich scale over a steel chassis so it turns both flame and rot. Owning this formula unlocks the Forge Dracolich-Scale Plate recipe.

components.name: Raw Timber
components.description: A freshly felled log, bark still on. Saw it into planks before it warps.

components.name: Raw Hide
components.description: An untanned animal hide, stiff and raw. Tan it before it spoils.

components.name: Flax Stalks
components.description: Cut flax, ready to be retted into fibre or woven into cloth.

components.name: Sand
components.description: A measure of clean silica sand for glassmaking.

components.name: Coal
components.description: Black coal that burns hot and long â€” fuel for the smelting furnace.

components.name: Copper Ore
components.description: Orange-streaked copper ore, ready for the furnace.

components.name: Tin Ore
components.description: Pale tin ore â€” alloyed with copper it makes bronze.

components.name: Raw Bone
components.description: Clean animal bone, good for meal, glue, or tool stock.

components.name: Charcoal
components.description: Charcoal burned from hardwood â€” a cleaner, hotter fuel than raw coal.

components.name: Copper Ingot
components.description: A bar of smelted copper.

components.name: Tin Ingot
components.description: A bar of smelted tin.

components.name: Bronze Ingot
components.description: Bronze, alloyed from copper and tin â€” harder than either alone.

components.name: Bone Meal
components.description: Ground bone, used as flux, fertiliser, and an alchemical base.

components.name: Iron Rivets
components.description: A handful of forged iron rivets and fasteners for armour and repairs.

components.name: Marshlight Glowcap
components.description: A bell-capped fungus that opens only after dusk, shedding a faint blue-green light. It grows on drowned wood in the marsh channels and on timber props in the deep galleries, and herbalists prize its restorative gleam as a base for poultices and tonics. Picked in daylight it is a grey, inert lump â€” the glow, and the virtue, come out at night.

components.name: Peat Brick
components.description: A cut and dried brick of bog peat from the Greywater channels. It burns slower and dirtier than coal but burns all the same, and frontier smiths keep a stack by the forge for when the coal-carts don't come. Cutting it is honest, filthy work â€” and misery in the rain.

components.name: Amber Pine-Resin
components.description: Thick, honey-coloured resin tapped from the old pines along the forest verge. Boiled down it makes a binding glue that grips better than wet sinew â€” fletchers seat arrowheads with it, and bowyers seal hafts and grips against the weather.

components.name: Formula: Mordant-Etched Dagger
components.description: Monster-research notes on crafting the Mordant-Etched Dagger. Owning this formula unlocks the recipe.

components.name: Formula: Acid-Proof Coat
components.description: Monster-research notes on crafting the Acid-Proof Coat. Owning this formula unlocks the recipe.

components.name: Formula: Belcher's Maw Bomb

components.name: Net
components.description: When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a Net. Target a creature you can see within 15 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or have the &Reference[Restrained] condition until it escapes. The target succeeds automatically if it is Huge or larger.To escape, the target or a creature within 5 feet of it must take an action to make a [[/skill ath 10]] check, freeing the Restrained creature on a success. Destroying the Net (AC 10; 5 HP; Immunity to Bludgeoning, Poison, and Psychic damage) also frees the target, ending the effect.

gatheringRealms.name: The Stonecrown Range
gatheringRealms.description: Iron-veined uplands and worked mine galleries on the southern frontier. Hard rock, honest ore, and the ordinary perils of going underground. The Old War-Road climbs its passes, and the deepest seams break through into the Undervein Galleries.

gatheringRealms.name: The Forest's Edge
gatheringRealms.description: Settled wilderness â€” old-growth woodland, hedgerow, and pasture where wood, hide, and fibre are taken. The Hedgerow Commons lie along its verge, and the Old War-Road runs the length of it.

gatheringRealms.name: The Mordant Hills
gatheringRealms.description: Acid-warm foothills riddled with balehound dens and iron seams. The deeper tunnels are markedly more dangerous, and the worst of them open into the Undervein Galleries shared with the Stonecrown mines.

gatheringRealms.name: The Blackwater Mire
gatheringRealms.description: A submerged forest of dark water and lichen-shadowed reeds where jadmÃ³r nest in the deep channels. The slow Greywater Channels drain it eastward toward the Bonemarsh.

gatheringRealms.name: The Bonemarsh
gatheringRealms.description: A drowned battlefield where Kuuvalgus was undone and unmade more than once. Bone-dust silts the water and the lesser dead still walk; the deeper you go, the worse it gets. The Greywater Channels feed it from the Blackwater side.

modifiers.note: Restless dead make the Bonemarsh especially dangerous.

gatheringRealms.name: The Ashen Field
gatheringRealms.description: An old battlefield gone to ash and bramble. Broken arms and armour lie thick under the turf â€” good scavenging for anyone willing to brave the things that still linger after dark. The Old War-Road that fed the war still crosses it.

recipes.name: Fletch a Batch of Crossbow Bolts
Cast a Batch of Sling Bullets

gatheringEnvironments.name: The Deepvein Mine
gatheringEnvironments.description: A working iron mine cut into the southern flank of the Stonecrown Range. No acid, no balehounds â€” just rock, ore, and the ordinary perils of going underground.

gatheringEnvironments.name: Forest's Edge
gatheringEnvironments.description: The settled wilderness â€” old-growth woodland, hedgerow, and pasture. Wood, leather, and fibre come from here, and so do the wolves and the toll-takers.

gatheringEnvironments.name: The Mordant Tunnels
gatheringEnvironments.description: A network of acid-warm caverns and bubbling mordant pools beneath the eastern foothills. Balehounds den here; iron-rich seams run through the walls.

gatheringEnvironments.name: The Blackwater Mire
gatheringEnvironments.description: A submerged forest of dark water, peat islands, and lichen-shadowed reeds. JadmÃ³r nest in the deep channels; parasites swarm beneath the surface.

gatheringEnvironments.name: The Bonemarsh
gatheringEnvironments.description: Drowned ground over the bones of a dracolich that would not stay dead. Bone-dust, soul-ash, and the shamble of the lesser dead reward those who sift carefully â€” and punish those who linger after dark.

gatheringEnvironments.name: The Ashen Field
gatheringEnvironments.description: A grave-field of a forgotten war. Scavengers pull rivets, bone, and broken arms from the turf â€” and sometimes wake what was buried with them.

gatheringEnvironments.name: The Old War-Road
gatheringEnvironments.description: The cobbled supply road that once fed the war, threading the Forest's Edge, climbing the Stonecrown passes, and crossing the Ashen Field. Wagon-wrecks, way-camps, and verge-meadows make for good gleaning â€” and the road has never stopped collecting tolls.

gatheringEnvironments.name: The Undervein Galleries
gatheringEnvironments.description: The deep seams where the Stonecrown mine-workings and the Mordant tunnels break into one another. The richest ore on the frontier runs through these galleries â€” under the worst air, the loosest stone, and things that hunt by ear.

gatheringEnvironments.name: The Greywater Channels
gatheringEnvironments.description: The slow grey waterways that drain the Blackwater Mire eastward into the Bonemarsh. Peat-cutters and herb-pickers work the channel banks by punt â€” the safest water in the marshes, which is not the same as safe.

gatheringEnvironments.name: The Hedgerow Commons
gatheringEnvironments.description: The settled verge between the Forest's Edge and the Stonecrown foothills â€” hedgerow, orchard-gone-wild, and common pasture. Herbs, flax, resin, and small game for anyone with a basket and a free afternoon.

tasks.name: Extract Iron Ore
tasks.description: Work a marked iron seam with a pick, chasing the vein where it runs richest. The bread-and-butter of any mine â€” reliable iron, with coal and copper turning up alongside it, and the odd seam that pays out in clean ingot.

tasks.name: Quarry Open Seam
tasks.description: Break and haul rough stone from an exposed face rather than chasing a vein. Heavier, blunter work than mining ore â€” it yields building stone and sand, with the occasional streak of iron or tin in the spoil.

tasks.name: Fell a Tree
tasks.description: Drop a sound tree, limb it, and buck it into workable lengths. The forest's staple yield â€” planks and raw timber for every haft, bow-stave, and building, with bark-fibre string as a bonus for the patient.

tasks.name: Hunt Deer for Hide
tasks.description: Track and take a deer for its hide, bone, and sinew. Deer move at the edges of the day, so a hunter who works dawn or dusk comes home with the better hide.

tasks.name: Snare Game-Birds
tasks.description: Set snares along the hedgerows and wait out the morning rise for grouse and woodcock. Light, quiet work that pays in fletching feathers and cord â€” and pays best at dawn, when the birds are on the wing.

tasks.name: Harvest Wild Flax
tasks.description: Pull wild flax from the pasture margins by the armful, ready for retting into fibre. The cloth-and-cordage crop â€” stalks, string, and the occasional finished bolt for anyone with a weaver's patience.

tasks.name: Hunt a Balehound
tasks.description: Run down one of the acid-dogs in its own tunnels and put it down before the pack answers. Dangerous, draining work â€” but a clean kill yields teeth, ear-cartilage, and the prized mordant that starts half the craft loop.

tasks.name: Forage Mordant Pools
tasks.description: Skim raw mordant from the bubbling acid-warm pools where the balehounds den, decanting it before it eats through your jars. No fight, but a steady nerve and a careful hand â€” the reward is mordant and the rare intact gland.

tasks.name: Hunt a JadmÃ³r
tasks.description: Wade the deep channels to take a jadmÃ³r where it nests, then harvest it fast before the carcass spoils. Venomous, draining, and slow to replenish â€” but the fangs, scale-plates, and venom sacs are worth the mire.

tasks.name: Forage Venom-Pickled Reeds
tasks.description: Cut the lichen-shadowed reeds that grow where jadmÃ³r venom has steeped the water, handling them with gloved care. The reeds â€” and the bile that pools at their roots â€” are alchemist's stock for anyone brave enough to gather them.

tasks.name: Sift the Bonemarsh
tasks.description: Wade the drowned graves and sift the bone-silt for what the dracolich's death left behind. The rarest forage on the frontier â€” withered sinew, bonemaw fangs, and precious soul-ash â€” taken from ground that does not want to give it up.

tasks.name: Scavenge the Battlefield
tasks.description: Dig broken arms and armour out of the turf where an old war was lost. Cheap raw stock for the repair-and-reforge trade â€” rivets and bone by the cartload, and sometimes a blade, shield, or hauberk worth saving. Safe by day; far less so after dark.

tasks.name: Glean the Old Road
tasks.description: Pick over the wagon-wrecks and way-camps strung along the old supply road. Lighter pickings than the battlefield proper â€” rivets, bone, and cord, with the odd battered shield left in a ditch by a fleeing quartermaster.

tasks.name: Delve the Deep Seams
tasks.description: Push past the worked galleries into the deep seams where the Stonecrown and Mordant workings meet. The richest ore on the frontier â€” heavy iron, coal, copper, and tin, with a rare seam of vein-born steel â€” bought with hard, draining labour under the worst air below ground.

tasks.name: Cut Greywater Peat
tasks.description: Slice and stack bricks of bog peat from the channel banks, leaving them to dry into forge-fuel. Filthy, honest work â€” and misery in the rain, which slumps the cut and ruins the drying. A storm makes it barely worth the trip.

tasks.name: Pick Marshlight Glowcaps
tasks.description: Gather the luminous bell-caps that open on drowned wood and gallery props once the light fails. They can only be taken after dusk â€” by day they are grey, inert lumps â€” and yield best at full night, when the glow, and the virtue, are strongest.

tasks.name: Forage Hedgerow Simples
tasks.description: Walk the hedgerows and commons with a basket, taking medicinal herbs, flax, and a little tapped resin as you go. Gentle, low-stamina work for any free afternoon â€” and the herbs come up best when picked at dawn, before the dew burns off.

tasks.name: Tap Amber Pine-Resin
tasks.description: Score the old pines along the verge and hang pots to catch the slow amber bleed. A bowyer's and fletcher's errand â€” thick binding resin for seating arrowheads and sealing hafts, with a little cut timber from the trees you work.

events.name: Sudden Downpour
events.description: The sky opens without warning. Everything is soaked â€” kindling, fletching, tempers â€” and the trails turn to soup.

events.name: Surveyor's Mark
events.description: An old surveyor's blaze, half-grown over â€” three notches and an arrow. Someone marked a cache, a claim, or a warning here, long before you came.

events.name: Curious Locals
events.description: A swineherd, a charcoal-burner's lad, or a wandering pedlar stops to watch you work â€” full of questions, gossip, and opinions on how you're doing it wrong.

events.name: Snapped Strap
events.description: A pack-strap, haft-binding, or boot-lace gives way at the worst moment. Nothing dangerous â€” just time lost and knuckles skinned making it hold.

events.name: Fog-Bound
events.description: The fog closes in until the world is ten paces wide. Sounds carry wrong, landmarks vanish, and the way back is suddenly a matter of opinion.

events.name: Wolves on the Trail
events.description: A small wolf pack scents the kill and follows at the edge of sight, patient as winter, until challenged or fed.

events.name: Bandit Toll
events.description: A boorish lot waits where the track narrows, levying their own toll on anyone carrying anything worth carrying.

events.name: Sinking Ground
events.description: What looked like solid footing is a skin of moss over black water. Gear goes under fast; people, slower â€” if they're lucky.

events.name: Rockslide
events.description: Scree shifts somewhere above and the slope lets go â€” a rattling grey river of stone between you and the path home.

events.name: Cave-In
events.description: A loose ceiling section gives way as you work, filling the gallery with dust and the sound of the mountain settling its accounts.

events.name: Foul Air
events.description: An unmarked vein of gas seeps from a sidewall â€” odorless, heavier than breath, and suffocating before anyone thinks to ask why the lamp went strange.

events.name: Claim-Jumpers
events.description: Another crew has been working your stretch under cover of dark â€” and they've left a lookout who'd rather you didn't report it.

events.name: Parasite Infestation
events.description: The water you have been wading through carries jadmÃ³r parasites â€” pale threads that find any broken skin and make themselves at home.

events.name: Mire Rot
events.description: A festering scratch from earlier in the day starts to fever. The marsh gets into wounds the way water gets into boots.

events.name: Acid-Spray Ambush
events.description: A previously unspotted balehound vents mordant from cover â€” a hissing arc of it across stone, gear, and anyone too slow.

events.name: Rival Scavengers
events.description: You are not the only crew working this ground tonight â€” and the other lot count anything already dug up as theirs.

events.name: Pack Ambush
events.description: A coordinated balehound pack closes the side passages behind you, patient and silent, herding you toward the den.

events.name: JadmÃ³r Ambush
events.description: A submerged adult breaks the surface as you work â€” a wall of green scale and patient hunger between you and the bank.

events.name: Night Terrors
events.description: Something paces you in the dark beyond the lamplight. It never quite shows itself â€” but it wants you to know it is there.

events.name: Restless Dead
events.description: The bone-dust stirs and the drowned dead rise to defend their master's grave â€” slow, sodden, and entirely too many.

events.name: Grave-Wights
events.description: The scavenging wakes something that does not care to be disturbed â€” and it remembers how to use the sword it was buried with.

events.name: Wasting Chill
events.description: A cold that is not weather seeps into the bones and will not leave. Fires don't touch it; only distance from this place does.

events.name: The Marsh Remembers
events.description: The water goes mirror-still and the marsh dreams aloud â€” drowned banners, a dragon's voice under the silt, the night Kuuvalgus was unmade. Those who listen too long wake somewhere else, or not at all.
